message:
feat(ft-conformance): add stream-size bounds checking and structured error formatting for legacy oracle

Add explicit stdout/stderr byte-count validation before processing legacy
oracle output.  Previously, an oracle script that emitted unbounded output
could cause large allocations or confusing errors downstream.

Changes:
- Introduce MAX_LEGACY_ORACLE_STDOUT_BYTES (1 MiB) and
  MAX_LEGACY_ORACLE_STDERR_BYTES (256 KiB) constants to cap acceptable
  oracle output sizes.
- New `validate_legacy_oracle_stream_bounds()` function fails fast with a
  clear diagnostic when either stream exceeds its limit.
- Extract `format_legacy_oracle_exit_error()` to produce bounded stderr
  diagnostics using the existing `bounded_diagnostic()` helper, avoiding
  unbounded error messages when oracle stderr is large.
- Add LEGACY_ORACLE_STDERR_DIAGNOSTIC_BYTES constant (256 bytes) to
  control how much of stderr is included in error messages.
- Refactor the `run_legacy_oracle_script()` caller to use the new
  validation and formatting helpers instead of inlining error construction.
- Add three unit tests covering oversized stdout rejection, oversized
  stderr rejection, and stderr diagnostic truncation behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
